F arn P olrrrcal P RACTTcBs C oMMrs s roN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
llu/ry 3,2001
Richard L. Poland
Law Offrces of Richard L. Poland
333 W. Broadway, Suite 200
Long Beach, CA 90802
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-123
Dear Mr. Poland
Re:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r This advice is based upon the facts as
provided in your request letter.2 Please note that this letter should not be construed to
evaluate any conduct that has akeady taken place. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) )
QUESTTON
With respect to an ad in the Yellow Pages for your private law practice that also
contains the words "VOTE" and "Office of the Long Beach City Prosecutor," an office
for which you are a candidate, should the payment for this ad be made from your
campaign bank account or from your private account for your law firm?
CONCLUSION
Because payment for the ad constitutes a campaign expenditure under the Act, it
must be paid for from your campaign bank account.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commissionregulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 1 0 9 - 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 This advice is applicable and confers immmity only to the extent that the facts provided to us are
correct, and that all of the material facts have been disclosed. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71;
$ 83114.)
ffi:fi=-


File No. A-01-123
Page No. 2
FACTS
You have provided a copy of your business advertisement that is running on the
cover of the Pacific Bell Smart Yellow Pages for the "Greater Long Beach & Lakewood
Area." This ad is ostensibly for your private law practice and is denominated as
"RICHARD L. POLAND ATTORNEY atLAW," but it also contains in large print, the
words "VOTE" and "Office of the Long Beach Clty Prosecutor." The word "VOTE"
appears as an acronym for several nonpolitical legal services provided by your private
law firm. The words describing these nonpolitical services are attached to the word,
"VOTE," which provides the first letter for each advertised service provided by your
firm. For example, the letter "V" in "VOTE" starts the phrase "VEIICULARACCIDENTS,"
and so forth. The letters in the word "VOTE" appear in such a manner that the acronym
is readily apparerfi. The words "Office of the Long Beach City Prosecutor" are also
readily apparent in the ad.
You are a candidate for the Office of Long Beach City Prosecutor for the election
in April of 2002. Your intent in running the above described advertisement was to
advertise your private law practice to potential clients, as well as to influence voters to
vote for you in the April 2002 election.
ANALYSIS
Under regulation I8225(a)(I), the definition of "expenditure" covers any payment
made for "the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters
for or against the nomination or election of a candidate." As your ad is aimed at swaying
voters to support your candidacy, it constitutes an expenditure.
Your payment for the ad would also be an expenditure, as a payment by a
candid'ate under Regulation 15225(a)(2)(A) A payment by a candidate is not an
"expenditure," where "it is clear from surrounding circumstances that the payment was
made for personal purposes unrelated to his or her candidacy...." (Ibid., emphasis
added.) In this case it cannot be said that the ad is unrelated to your candidacy. In fact,
there is no question that the payment for the ad is related to your candidacy, and again,
constitutes an expenditure.3
Because the payment for the ad is a campaign expenditure, it must be made from
your campaign bank account under $ 85201(e). To the extent that personal or business
funds would be used to pay for the ad, they would have to be deposited in the campaign
account prior to making the expenditure under $ 85201(d). (See also Regulation
1821s(bx2).)
' With the conclusion of the election, the payment for the ad witl no longer be related to your
candidacy. (Rosenthal Advice Letter, No. I-93-167.)


File No. A-01-123
Page No. 3
If you have any other questions regarding this malter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
lr.,
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
